items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been highly active in early 2026, releasing a rapid succession of GPT-5 model variants. The flagship GPT-5.4 was released as OpenAI's most capable frontier model, combining advances in reasoning, coding, and agentic workflows. Notably, GPT-5.4 is the first general-purpose model with native computer-use capabilities, enabling agents to operate computers and carry out complex workflows across applications, with support for up to 1M tokens of context. items.title: OpenAI Models by OpenAI - Release Notes - March 2026 - Releasebot
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet: OpenAI has been on an aggressive release cadence in early 2026. GPT-5.4 launched in March 2026 across ChatGPT, the API, and Codex, featuring a 1 million token context window and 33% fewer factual errors compared to GPT-5.2. A higher-end GPT-5.4 Pro variant targets maximum performance and deeper reasoning on complex workloads. GPT-5.3 Instant and GPT-5.3 Instant Mini were also released, offering more natural conversation, richer web search results, and stronger contextual writing. GPT-5.4 mini is

items.title: OpenAI Is Having a Rough 2026 - And It Shows
items.author: autogpt.net
items.snippet: OpenAI has been active on Twitter/X in April 2026 with several major announcements. The company announced a massive $122 billion funding round, pushing its post-money valuation to approximately $852 billion and reporting $2 billion in monthly revenue. Sam Altman also confirmed via social channels that pretraining for OpenAI's next frontier model (internally codenamed 'Spud') completed on March 24, 2026, with a release expected within weeks. items.title: AI and International Security Pathways of Impact
items.author: cdn.openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been actively publishing research across multiple domains in early 2026. Key recent publications include work on AI safety and alignment: 'Evaluating Chain-of-Thought Monitorability' explores how reasoning model transparency can serve as an AI safety safeguard, while 'How We Monitor Internal Coding Agents for Misalignment' details chain-of-thought monitoring techniques for detecting misalignment in deployed coding agents.
